summary: Auto-Scaling & Priority Lanes feature tested and verified. Found and fixed 1 bug (MongoDB duplicate _id error). Backend APIs 100% functional. Frontend dashboard shows all components correctly.

issues: Bright yellow (#E5FF00) background on cards creates readability issues - consistent with iteration_82 feedback

bug_fixed.root_cause: Line 610 in priority_scaling_service.py was setting '_id: None' in insert_one() which causes duplicate key error
bug_fixed.fix_applied: Changed from `{**scaling_event, '_id': None}` to `scaling_event.copy()` to let MongoDB auto-generate _id

critical_code_review_comments: Auto-scaling engine starts on server startup and runs every 10 seconds
All scaling rules are correctly configured (high_queue_depth, very_high_queue_depth, high_latency, premium_queue_growing, low_queue_depth, very_low_queue_depth)
Priority lanes have correct SLAs: Enterprise 30s, Pro 60s, Basic 120s, Free 300s
Manual scaling correctly enforces min/max worker limits
Admin-only endpoints properly require get_admin_user dependency
seed_data_creation: None needed - used existing admin user
context_for_next_testing_agent: Auto-Scaling and Priority Lanes fully functional. Bug fixed in priority_scaling_service.py (MongoDB _id issue). All 6 scaling rules active. 4 priority tiers configured correctly.

backend_endpoints.returns: current_workers, min_workers, max_workers, running, active_rules, queue_stats
scaling config, priority_lanes (4 tiers), queue_by_tier, recent_scaling_events

frontend_components.component: Worker Auto-Scaling card with Current Workers display
Priority Lanes card with 4 tiers (Free, Basic, Pro, Enterprise)
